question: What is radiochemical stability?
options: How stable the radioisotope is physically
Resistance of the radiolabeled compound to decomposition or radionuclide loss in biological conditions
The chemical stability of the peptide only
The physical half-life of the isotope only
explanation: Radiochemical stability ensures the radionuclide remains securely chelated throughout its journey in vivo. Instability causes non-specific uptake in bone or liver.
